name: Kanda Catholic Church
description: One of the oldest Catholic churches in Tokyo, founded in 1874. It is a registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan and serves as a significant spiritual landmark in the Kanda district.
mission_statement: To proclaim the Gospel and serve the spiritual needs of the city.

name: Japan Evangelical Lutheran Tokyo Church
description: Located in the bustling Shinjuku area, this church is the flagship of the Japan Evangelical Lutheran Church. It features a stunning pipe organ and a strong focus on community music and service.
mission_statement: Saved by Grace through Faith.

name: Christ Church Yokohama
description: A historic international Anglican congregation located on the famous Yamate bluff overlooking the harbor. It has served the expatriate and local English-speaking community for over 150 years.
mission_statement: To be a beacon of Christ's love in the port city.

name: Tokyo Central Church
description: A central Methodist congregation with a deep focus on social holiness and urban mission in the Shinjuku district. It hosts numerous community programs for seniors and youth.
mission_statement: Leading people into a transformed life in Christ.

name: Asakusa Catholic Church
description: A historic parish located in the traditional Asakusa district, dedicated to the North American Martyrs. It serves a diverse population including many migrant families.
mission_statement: Worshipping together in the heart of old Tokyo.

name: Takanawa Catholic Church
description: Known for its monument to the Edo Martyrs, this church commemorates the history of faith in Japan. It is located near Shinagawa Station and has a large, active community.
mission_statement: Commemorating the martyrs, living for Christ.

name: Koenji Catholic Church
description: A welcoming community in the Suginami area known for its vibrant parish life and support for local refugees. It hosts various international cultural exchange events.
mission_statement: Walking together in faith and love.

name: Den-en-chofu Catholic Church
description: A beautiful Franciscan parish in the Den-en-chofu residential area, featuring a traditional garden and architecture. It is known for its quiet, prayerful atmosphere.
mission_statement: Peace and Goodness.

name: Shibuya Evangelical Church
description: An evangelical congregation located in the heart of Shibuya, focusing on outreach to students and young professionals. It emphasizes personal discipleship and Bible study.
mission_statement: Bringing the light of the Gospel to the city center.

name: Osaka Central Church
description: A primary United Church of Christ in Japan (UCCJ) congregation in Osaka, with a long history of social activism and welfare programs. It remains a key hub for Protestantism in the Kansai region.
mission_statement: Serving Osaka with the love of Christ.

name: Tamatsukuri Catholic Cathedral
description: Also known as St. Mary's Cathedral of Osaka, it is the seat of the Archdiocese. The church is famous for its pipe organ and beautiful modernist architecture.
mission_statement: To be the spiritual heart of the Osaka Archdiocese.

name: Toyonaka Catholic Church
description: A thriving parish in the northern suburbs of Osaka, known for its strong Sunday School and family-oriented ministries. It serves as a community center for the local residential area.
mission_statement: Building a family of faith in Toyonaka.

name: Kyoto Resurrection Church
description: A central Anglican parish in Kyoto with a focus on liturgy and traditional Japanese Christian expression. It is part of the Diocese of Kyoto (NSKK).
mission_statement: Witnessing to the Resurrection in the ancient capital.

name: Nara Christ Church
description: A historic Anglican church located near the famous Nara Park. The building is a registered Tangible Cultural Property and combines Western and Japanese architectural elements.
mission_statement: Peace in the heart of Nara.

name: Nagoya All Saints Church
description: The primary Anglican presence in Nagoya, focusing on traditional worship and community outreach. It is known for its beautiful brick facade and historic pipe organ.
mission_statement: All are welcome in the name of the Lord.

name: Sendai Orthodox Church
description: The Church of the Annunciation serves the Orthodox community in Northern Japan. Rebuilt after historical fires, it remains a center for Byzantine liturgy and spiritual life in Tohoku.
mission_statement: Proclaiming the eternal truth of Orthodoxy.

name: Nagoya Orthodox Church
description: The Church of the Epiphany is a serene place of worship in Nagoya, featuring traditional iconography and a dedicated choir for Byzantine chant.
mission_statement: Illumination through the Holy Spirit.

name: Osaka Orthodox Church
description: A focal point for Orthodox Christianity in the Kansai region, this church provides regular services in Japanese and Russian. It is known for its resilient community.
mission_statement: Witnessing the ancient faith in modern Osaka.

name: Kyoto Orthodox Church
description: The Church of the Holy Resurrection in Kyoto is the oldest Orthodox church building in Japan. Its design follows traditional Russian Orthodox architecture with a Japanese touch.
mission_statement: Ancient faith in an ancient city.

name: Toyohashi Orthodox Church
description: A designated Important Cultural Property of Japan, this church is famous for its intricate wood carvings and early icons by Yamashita Rin.
mission_statement: Faith preserved in stone and wood.

name: Shizuoka Orthodox Church
description: A local Orthodox community serving the Shizuoka region with regular liturgical services and community study groups.
mission_statement: Searching for Truth in Shizuoka.

name: Yokohama Orthodox Church
description: Located on the historic Yamate hill, this church serves the Orthodox residents of Yokohama and the greater Kanagawa area. It has strong ties to the early mission history.
mission_statement: Bearing witness to the harbor of souls.

name: Tokyo Musashino Church
description: A vibrant United Church of Christ in Japan (UCCJ) congregation in Western Tokyo, known for its focus on social justice, environmental stewardship, and community welfare.
mission_statement: A community of faith serving the Musashino region.

name: Akita Seitai Hoshikai
description: Also known as Our Lady of Akita, this convent and chapel is a major pilgrimage site famous for the reported weeping of a statue of Mary. It focuses on prayer and penance.
mission_statement: Praying for the world in the spirit of Akita.

name: Shimonoseki Catholic Church
description: A central parish in the port city of Shimonoseki, serving a local and seafaring international community. It is known for its beautiful hilltop location.
mission_statement: Grace at the gates of the Strait.

name: Aizu-Wakamatsu Catholic Church
description: A historic parish in the samurai city of Aizu, focusing on education and local community service in Fukushima prefecture.
mission_statement: Faith and courage in Aizu.

name: Takatsuki Catholic Church
description: Located in the city of the Blessed Justo Takayama Ukon, this church is a center for pilgrimage and historical study of the 'Kirishitan' era.
mission_statement: Remembering the Justo, following the Lord.

name: Tottori Catholic Church
description: A small but dedicated community serving the Tottori region on the Sea of Japan coast. It is known for its close-knit family atmosphere.
mission_statement: A small light in the sand dunes.

name: Matsumoto Catholic Church
description: Serving the Matsumoto region near the historic castle, this parish has a strong focus on liturgical arts and social welfare.
mission_statement: Grace in the Alps.

name: Nagano Catholic Church
description: The primary Catholic presence in Nagano city, providing a home for diverse local and migrant believers in the mountainous region.
mission_statement: One heart in the Nagano mountains.
